text: A 35-year-old man undergoes multiple invasive investigations for chronic symptoms with no organic cause. He deliberately seeks these tests. Likely diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Factitious Disorder involves intentional symptom fabrication to assume the sick role.

text: A 3-year-old child presents with a painful elbow held in pronation after a traction injury (Nursemaid's elbow). Diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Nursemaid's Elbow (Radial head subluxation) is common after pulling a child's arm.

text: A trauma patient has pelvic fractures, blood at the meatus, and a high-riding prostate. Next best step?
rationale: Dx: Urethral Injury. Perform Retrograde Urethrography before any catheterization.

text: A newborn is cyanotic. Oxygenation does not improve significantly with 100% oxygen. Likely cause?
rationale: Dx: Transposition of the Great Vessels (TGA) presents with cyanosis and poor O2 response.

text: A 68-year-old man with severe back pain and a pulsatile abdominal mass. Best test to confirm?
rationale: Dx: Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm (AAA). CT or Ultrasound is the gold standard for diagnosis.
